components.role: Gyrator amplifier — simulates an inductor using capacitor and resistor feedback to create a resonant bandpass
Input/series resistor — sets the bandwidth and boost amount of the resonant peak
Gyrator resistor — with C1, simulates inductance L = R2 * C1 * R3
Gyrator ground resistor — sets the equivalent inductance value
Gyrator capacitor — core of the simulated inductor
Boost amount control — varies the depth of the mid-frequency peak
Frequency control — tunes the center frequency of the boost (optional, replaces R2)

dsp_model.description: Parametric EQ band — second-order bandpass/peaking filter centered at tunable mid frequency

behavioral_description: A gyrator-based mid-frequency boost that uses an op-amp to simulate an inductor, creating a resonant peak at a tunable center frequency. This is the heart of many parametric EQ pedals and mid-boost circuits. The gyrator avoids the size, weight, and cost of a real inductor while providing equivalent resonant behavior. The center frequency can be swept from low-mids (200Hz — thick, chunky) through upper-mids (2-5kHz — cutting, present). The Q parameter controls bandwidth: low Q gives a broad, gentle boost across a wide range; high Q gives a narrow, focused peak that can make a guitar voice cut through a dense mix. This is essential for sculpting overdrive and distortion tones, pushing specific frequency ranges into clipping while leaving others clean.
